mnemonics: Fill Knapsack For Optimal Packing (FKFOP): A mnemonic for remembering the goal is to optimally fill the knapsack.
keyPoints: The Knapsack Problem aims to select a subset of items with maximum total value, without exceeding the weight capacity.
Memory Functions (or memoization) optimize the recursive solution by storing results of solved subproblems to avoid recomputation.
The solution uses a 2D DP table where dp[i][w] represents the maximum value achievable with the first 'i' items and a weight limit 'w'.
commonMistakes: Confusing the 0/1 Knapsack (items cannot be broken) with the Fractional Knapsack (items can be broken), which has a different, greedy solution.
Incorrectly initializing the DP table, especially the first row and column which represent base cases (0 items or 0 capacity).
Off-by-one errors when mapping the item index 'i' to its position in the list or DP table, often forgetting that indexing typically starts at 1 in the algorithm.
